rationale_by_leg.claude: Exceptionally faithful and skeptical on the GPT-5.6 Sol usage-burn saga: it captures the 'no nerfing' pushback and, crucially, Theo's own walkback of the flat-2x-billing claim (snapshot line 35075) — the single best-executed story in either brief. It also nails the SWE-Atlas hype as Polymarket/Musk self-amplification. But it misses the cycle's biggest breaking item (Musk ordering the SpaceXAI data wipe, ~8.6K likes), the heavily-covered Muse Spark 1.1 release, and the Anthropic 300K-values study. The 12:00 section is orphaned — absent from both the Telegram summary and headline alerts — and public_items=5 undercounts the shown items.
rationale_by_leg.zai-glm-5p2: Broader, fresher coverage of the actual 22:00 signal: leads with the SpaceXAI data-wipe (verified, highest-engagement item) and covers Artifacts, the Anthropic values study, Muse Spark, Antigravity, and the open-weight gateway share. Faithful throughout with sharp caveats (ZDR walkback, self-reported Muse Spark, unresolved causality, unconfirmed talent move). Format is internally consistent — Telegram, headlines, and public_items=9 all match. Minor nit: 'roughly 5.9K likes' for Musk's post vs 8,638 in the snapshot, and it skips the GPT-5.6 saga entirely.

Broad, fresh coverage of the actual 22:00 window: leads with the day's biggest item (Musk/SpaceXAI data wipe, 8,638 likes), plus the fresh Claude Artifacts launch, Anthropic values study, Muse Spark, Antigravity, DFlash, open-weight gateway share. All spot-checks trace exactly to snapshot, including the secondary '#3 Debate Benchmark behind Fable 5 & Opus 4.7' detail and Anthropic warmth/rigor axis. Per-story verify tags, counter-hypotheses, and a skeptic's corner on the unconfirmed talent-move. Single clean same-hour section; headlines map 1:1 to stories; status count (9) matches. Knock: omits the Codex-nerf saga.
rationale_by_leg.claude: Superb depth and source hygiene on its two stories — the Codex 'nerf' counter-analysis (Theo's self-walkback, Sottiaux's dispute) and two well-reasoned skeptic items are best-in-class, all faithful. But it ships a full stale '12:00 UTC' section (all 01:00-09:00 tweets) inside a 22:00 run, and its fresh-cycle coverage is thin: it omits the day's single biggest story (SpaceXAI data wipe), Meta's Muse Spark, and the Anthropic values study. Its own #1 story (Codex nerf) is absent from the headline alerts, and public_items=5 doesn't match visible content.
